data.content:
The file is generated successfully but never added to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the `.cs` file but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn never sees it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The file is being generated (`Generated code file: obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't know about it. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the file to the `Compile` item group.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` items using an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target, after the file is written:


Adding the generated file to compilation...

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and the app runs correctly.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. The generated file was invisible to the C# compiler.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` item inside the target so the generated source is included in compilation.
